sections.title: Starting off
Talking to Jamila
Talking to Jamilia again before trying on the ring

lines.speaker: Jamila
lines.text: Hello there. Looking to buy some of my wares?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About that special item...

lines.speaker: Jamila
lines.text: Oh I think you're going to love it!

sections.title: Talking to Jamila after getting the message once

lines.speaker: Jamila
lines.text: Hello there. Looking to buy some of my wares? I also have a special item in stock that I reckon you'll love!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: About that special item...

lines.speaker: Jamila
lines.text: Yes, I'm sorry the ring didn't fit. Still, nothing lost and nothing gained, right?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe I should try it on again.

lines.speaker: Jamila
lines.text: Well, I suppose you can. But first, you should make some room to carry it.

lines.speaker: Jamila
lines.text: Fair enough.

lines.speaker: Jamila
lines.text: Still too small I'm afraid. Now, it's time for me to get on with my work. Come back soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But what about...

lines.speaker: Jamila
lines.text: Sorry, but I have a lot to do. Come back soon.

lines.speaker: Jamila
lines.text: Hello there. Looking to buy some of my wares? I also have a special item in stock that I reckon you'll love!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's this special item?

lines.speaker: Jamila
lines.text: Oh... I seem to have misplaced it. Sorry about that. If you come back later, I might have found it.

lines.speaker: Jamila
lines.text: Oh it's fantastic! However, before we go into the details, you should make some room to carry it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Make some room? But I've not even decided if I want this... whatever it is.

lines.speaker: Jamila
lines.text: I just know that once you see it, you'll want it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay... I'll be back soon then.

lines.speaker: Jamila
lines.text: Oh it's fantastic! It's an exclusive item just for you! Consider it a thank you for all you've done for our city.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, let's have a look.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A ring?

lines.speaker: Jamila
lines.text: Isn't it wonderful! Here, try it on!

lines.speaker: Jamila
lines.text: Ah, it seems the ring is too small. Never mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait... what?

lines.speaker: Jamila
lines.text: Well, time for me to get on with my work. Come back soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for the offer, but I'll pass.

lines.speaker: Jamila
lines.text: Oh, I see. Well come back if you change your mind.

sections.title: Talking to Maisa
Talking to Maisa again before hearing about the strange happenings

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are these strange happenings you mentioned?

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Citizens from Menaphos have been disappearing. Guards as well. They've been leaving the city, despite the quarantine.

sections.title: Talking to Maisa again before the cutscene

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Ready to go and investigate the excavation?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to do something first.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Well don't take too long!

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Good, you made it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know you! You're Maisa, that spy from Al Kharid. But you're meant to be in Menaphos!

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: I told Osman I was done with that posting, and I meant it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But how did you get out of the city?

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: The same way I helped those citizens of yours. Even with the quarantine, Menaphos are still sending their dead to Sophanem.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you hid yourself among them? I see. Why are you out here though? And why did you send me that message?

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Because there's something very strange going on. I intend to work out what, but I need your help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why me?

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: My list of allies grows shorter every day, but you actually seem to care about what's happening out here. You also seem to know what you're doing.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Brave of you to assume that I ever actually know what I'm doing.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Well I'm sure you can't be worse than Osman. You might even have the guts to take some action if needed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right... so what are these strange happenings?

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Citizens from Menaphos have been disappearing. Guards as well. They've been leaving the city, despite the quarantine.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They've been bypassing the quarantine? How?

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: No idea, but I have managed to find out where they've been going.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where?

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: South of here, to the old necropolis. They're excavating something there, but I don't know what. There's also something strange going on with them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: I went to this excavation of theirs to have a look around, and the way they're acting is very unusual. It's like nothing I've seen before.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting. I think I need to see this for myself.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Well let me know when you're ready, and I'll take you down there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Look at them! How long have they been doing this?

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: I'm not sure, but they're at it both day and night.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So they never rest?

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Doesn't seem so.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How weird. Can we get a closer look?

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Should be able to. When I came to investigate before, I was able to walk right up to them. They didn't even seem to care. It was like they were in some sort of trance.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm going to have a look around.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: I'll keep watch here. Try to keep a low profile.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll try, but low profile isn't really one of my specialties.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Oh good. I'll prepare for violence then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to do something first.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Well don't take too long!

sections.title: Talking to Jamila after talking to Maisa

lines.speaker: Jamila
lines.text: Hello there. Looking to buy some of my wares?

sections.title: Talking to Maisa at the necropolis

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Found anything yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still looking.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Be careful. There's something creepy about all of this.

sections.title: Inspecting the blocked entry before finishing the cutscene
Inspecting the blocked entry after finishing the cutscene

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting. I'd better report back to Maisa.

sections.title: Talking to a Citizen

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: The work must be completed...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... this seems familiar...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... this seems familiar... I'd better report back to Maisa.

sections.title: Talking to a Menaphite Guard

lines.speaker: Menaphite Guard
lines.text: The work must be completed...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... this seems familiar...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... this seems familiar... I'd better report back to Maisa.

sections.title: Guard cutscene
Using an overhead prayer during the Head Menaphite Guard fight

lines.speaker: Head Menaphite Guard
lines.text: Your false gods will not protect you!

sections.title: Running away from the Head Menaphite Guard fight
Talking to Maisa after fleeing Head Menaphite Guard fight

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Come on. We'd better get back down there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh... Hello there.

lines.speaker: Head Menaphite Guard
lines.text: You should not be here...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Really? Why not?

lines.speaker: Head Menaphite Guard
lines.text: The work cannot be disturbed... the mistress commands it...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Mistress? You mean the Devourer! This is all her doing, isn't it? I'd recognise her work anywhere.

lines.speaker: Head Menaphite Guard
lines.text: You know too much... you must be removed...

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Looks like you need a hand.

lines.speaker: Head Menaphite Guard
lines.text: Die...

sections.title: Talking to Maisa after defeating the Head Menaphite Guard
Talking to Citizen after killing the Head Menaphite Guard

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: The work must be completed... the mistress commands it...

sections.title: Talking to Menaphite Guard after killing the Head Menaphite Guard

lines.speaker: Menaphite Guard
lines.text: The work must be completed... the mistress commands it...

sections.title: Inspecting the blocked entry after killing the Head Menaphite Guard

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Nice job keeping things low profile.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: To be fair, I did warn you.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: I suppose. Were you able to discover anything?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sort of. I still don't know why these people are here, but I do know who sent them.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Who?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Amascut, the Devourer.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: What? The goddess? You're joking!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't believe me?

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: It's just quite the claim. How do you know it's her?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: She has hypnotic powers. She's the one that put them in this trance! I know it better than most. I was unlucky enough to experience it myself back in Sophanem.

lines.speaker: Head Menaphite Guard
lines.text: Urgh...

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Perhaps this one can give us something more to go on.

lines.speaker: Head Menaphite Guard
lines.text: What's happening? Help me...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You were hypnotised, but you're free now. Do you remember anything about what happened to you?

lines.speaker: Head Menaphite Guard
lines.text: It's all so unclear... I can't remember...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know what you're going through. Try to think back to before you were hypnotised. What's the last thing you remember?

lines.speaker: Head Menaphite Guard
lines.text: We were told we had an audience with Pharaoh Kemesis, but when we arrived at the palace he wasn't there. It was just this woman.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: What woman?

lines.speaker: Head Menaphite Guard
lines.text: I don't know. I just remember her eyes, her deep red eyes...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's Amascut alright.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Do you remember anything after that?

lines.speaker: Head Menaphite Guard
lines.text: We came here to find something... I think. A tomb! We unblocked the entrance, and she went inside. There were others with her.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What does she want from the tomb?

lines.speaker: Head Menaphite Guard
lines.text: I don't know... We never saw the inside. The entrance collapsed again after she went in.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's okay. You've been a great help.

lines.speaker: Head Menaphite Guard
lines.text: Wait! I remember one more thing... there were these monsters helping her! They walked like humans, but they had the heads of insects!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That sounds like those scarab creatures from the tunnels below Sophanem.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Scabarites! Do you know where they came from?

lines.speaker: Head Menaphite Guard
lines.text: Some ruins... To the north east...

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: I think that's all we're learning from this one. I'll get him some medical attention. Meet me back at my camp.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will do.

sections.title: The Ruins of Ullek
Talking to Maisa
Talking to Maisa again before hearing about the leads

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what are these leads you mentioned?

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: First up we have Kemesis, Pharaoh of Menaphos.

sections.title: Searching the Camping Equipment

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: So that was... interesting. A hypnotising goddess wasn't quite what I expected.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You sound like you still don't believe me.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: You have to admit, it's a lot to take in. Still, I guess it doesn't change our next steps.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And what are they?

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: That guard gave us two leads to look into. Hopefully one of them will give us some answers as to what's so important about that tomb.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Should we reach out to Osman about this?

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Osman? Why? That man won't be of any help. He'd have happily left me to rot in Menaphos, just like how he sent Kaleef to his death!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Kaleef? He was the spy that was meant to relieve you, right? I thought you didn't even know him?

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: What? Oh, right.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Look, we don't need Osman. We can handle this ourselves.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fair enough... so what are our leads?

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: First up we have Kemesis, Pharaoh of Menaphos.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you know about him?

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Truth be told, not a lot. He's probably the most powerful man in the Kharidian Desert, but he's incredibly reclusive. He's rarely seen in public.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If he's under Amascut's control, that could be a big problem. Do you know how we could get to him?

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Even without the quarantine, it would be incredibly difficult. His palace is a fortress.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That doesn't sound hopeful.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: It isn't. While there may be ways out of Menaphos, I don't know of any way back in again, let alone a way into the Pharaoh's own palace.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We'll leave that lead for now then. What about the other one?

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: The guard mentioned some scabarites. Perhaps we can learn something from them. He said they came from some ruins north east of the necropolis.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That would put the ruins directly east of here. All I see are cliffs.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Maybe there's something on the other side of them?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Could be worth exploring, I guess.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Right, so we have our leads. One seems impossible to follow, but the other might have some promise. While I think about how we can access Menaphos, do you want to look into these ruins?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure, but where do you even plan to start?

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: If we're ever going to get close to the Pharaoh, the Menaphos quarantine has to end. Kemesis himself has made it clear that will only happen when the plagues in Sophanem are eradicated.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: From what I hear, they've managed to deal with most of them now. That just leaves the pox they're all infected with.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what, you're going to cure the pox?

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Exactly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Surely if it were that simple, the High Priest would have done it by now.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Have you met that man? He can try to present himself as a wise leader all he wants, but it's just a facade. The lizards around here have bigger brains than him, and that's probably still an insult to the lizards.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: He didn't even have the guts to get rid of the plagued animals. The guards had to put them down themselves. And it's not like they can do that to the citizens!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, they could. But I imagine that would be considered quite rude.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Anyway, I reckon I might have a chance of succeeding where the High Priest failed. I have an old friend in Nardah named Zahur. She's a herbalist. I think she might be able to help.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: While I go and visit her, you see if you can find these ruins. Remember, they should be just east of here. Meet me in Nardah once you have something.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, good luck.

Entry

Entering the ruins

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello! You must be the scabarites I'm looking for.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm guessing you're going to try and kill me now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll take the silence as a yes...

Pulling levers
Pulling the first lever
When the lever resets
Pulling the second lever
Getting hit by darts

If 4 emblems are in the plaque

Using an emblem on an empty urn

Using an emblem on a full urn
Pulling the lever by the urns
Opening the downstairs locked door
Opening the upstairs locked door
Talking to the Spirit
Talking to Mehhar again before learning about the tomb

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You can help? Do you know of this tomb?

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: Even when I still lived, the desert hid many secrets. Now, centuries later, it hides even more. Of all those secrets, few are greater than the ones hidden in that tomb.

sections.title: Talking to Mehhar again before hearing the incantation

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I need to get into your father's tomb and stop Amascut.

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: Before you leave, I ask that you remember your original purpose here.

sections.title: Talking to Mehhar again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again. How do I access the upper chamber?

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: You will find a key in an urn at the back of this room. May Scabaras guide you to success.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello?

lines.speaker: Spirit
lines.text: Curious... It has been a long time since I've spoken to another. The last time was when my body still had form. Tell me, stranger, what brings you to my tomb?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was looking for some scabarites.

lines.speaker: Spirit
lines.text: Ah, yes. The fallen remnants of my once great sect. You will find plenty of them here. Why do you seek them?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know of Amascut, the Devourer?

lines.speaker: Spirit
lines.text: All too well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's an old tomb south of here. Amascut has been excavating the entrance, and the scabarites have been helping her. She's looking for something inside, but I don't know what.

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: I see. If you are an enemy of the Devourer and her corrupt scabarites, then you are a friend to me. I am Mehhar, and I can help you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You can help? Do you know of this tomb?

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: Even when I still lived, the desert hid many secrets. Now, centuries later, it hides even more. Of all those secrets, few are greater than the ones hidden in that tomb.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Whose tomb is it?

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: It is the resting place of my father, Osmumten. He was once Pharaoh of these lands. My destiny was to succeed him, but the usurpers saw an end to that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And what are the secrets in his tomb?

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: Being a Pharaoh offers many luxuries, both in life and in death. Many strive only for these luxuries. They forget that being a Pharaoh also comes with responsibility.

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: My father had a responsibility greater than most. At the time of his rule, our people were locked in a great war. To hold back the Ruinous Powers, our gods granted us immensely powerful weapons.

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: When the war was over, my father was tasked with hiding those weapons, lest they fall into nefarious hands. Two of them were buried with him. They will be what the Devourer seeks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are these weapons?

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: I trust you are aware of what the Devourer desires?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Souls?

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: Yes. When the war ended and the Devourer rose from the ashes, she did so with only one goal. She will not stop until every soul, living or dead, has been destroyed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And these weapons can help her?

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: Many would call the Devourer a god, but they speak only half truths. She is a demi-goddess of limited power, and does not possess the means to devour souls on a mass scale.

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: The weapons in my father's tomb can be used to drain a being of divine energy and transfer that energy to another. If used correctly, they would grant the Devourer true godhood.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then I need to get into that tomb and stop her.

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: Before you leave, I ask that you remember your original purpose here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The scabarites?

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: Yes. They have made their home here in my tomb, disturbing my rest. From the chamber above, the High Priest of Scabaras commands them.

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: He believes he does so in the name of Scabaras. He does not realise that he is actually a puppet of the Devourer.

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: Free him of her control, and the Devourer will be without a powerful resource. It will also allow me to rest once more.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He might also know more about what Amascut is planning. I did free a guard from Amascut's control through combat. I could try that again.

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: When the Devourer first emerged, Icthlarin himself taught us an incantation to free an individual from her control. I would suggest you use it, rather than risking harm to the High Priest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, that seems wise. What's this incantation?

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: 'Majaan, Manand, Almuk.' Be warned, while it will work on those she has hypnotised, it will have no effect on her corrupted akhs. They are sadly beyond saving.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see. Thank you, Mehhar.

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: You will find a key to the upper chamber in an urn to the back of this room. Now go, with the blessing of Scabaras.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me more about yourself.

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: In life I was Mehhar, son of Osmumten. To me, my death was mere moments ago. To you on the other hand, I am surely considered ancient.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How ancient?

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: I sense that the gods have long since departed this world, but when I lived, they still walked among us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And your father, Osmumten? You said he was the Pharaoh. What happened?

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: My father ruled during the great war. While the war was eventually won, many considered the cost too great. My father led us with the support of the gods, yet he was the one people blamed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So they killed him?

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: Him, and the rest of my family. I would have died as well, were it not for the bravery of my father's servants. They gave their lives, so that I might live.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And then you came here?

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: To Ullek? Yes. I found solace here with a group dedicated to Scabaras. In time, I became their leader, and the Sect of Scabaras was formed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's not much left of Ullek now, or the sect.

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: That is no surprise. Such is the fate of all things. Still, while the sect would have eventually decayed, it was the Devourer's corruption that quickened its demise.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: Don't be. You have a chance to free the High Priest of Scabaras. If you do, the sect may yet be reborn.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you know about Amascut?

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: The one you call Amascut died long ago. In her place, the Devourer was born.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How did it happen?

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: Only Icthlarin himself knows the true answer to that question.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay... but you must have some idea?

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: During the great war, our forces were decimated by the servants of the Ruinous Powers. To turn the tide, Icthlarin and Amascut sought powerful allies to aid us.

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: They found them far from here, in a place beyond this world. A powerful race of faceless ones. They became Icthlarin's Stern Judges.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's that got to do with Amascut?

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: Upon her return, Amascut vanished. She played no further part in the war. Only when the fighting was over did she reappear, but it wasn't really her at all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you think it had something to do with the Stern Judges?

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: It seems highly likely.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me more about the war you mentioned.

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: It started during the rule of my grandmother. An ever-expanding empire to the north set their sights on our lands. War soon broke out.

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: The armies of the Ruinous Powers were far stronger than our own. That was until Icthlarin's Stern Judges joined the fray. Thanks to them, the war was seemingly won.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It wasn't really though, was it?

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: No. Among the Stern Judges, there was a serpent. After my father became Pharaoh, this serpent convinced the others to abandon us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They joined the enemy?

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: They did. We were powerless against a force of that might. Nothing could stop their advance.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how did the war end?

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: It was Tumeken himself. He sacrificed himself to annihilate the enemy. The cost was great, but the war was won.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So Tumeken died?

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: That's a matter of perspective. I died long ago, and yet we are still having this conversation.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That doesn't really answer my question.

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: While Tumeken himself may be gone, he lives on through his four avatars. While they walk among us, Tumeken will always be at our side.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd better get going.

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: Scabaras be with you.

sections.title: Searching the urn by Mehhar
The Champion of Scabaras

lines.speaker: High Priest of Scabaras
lines.text: Intruder! You are not welcome here!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fear not! I am here to rescue you!

lines.speaker: High Priest of Scabaras
lines.text: Kill him!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait! Majaan, Manand...

lines.speaker: High Priest of Scabaras
lines.text: Silence! Champion, protect your High Priest!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fine. We'll do it your way.

sections.title: Talking to Mehhar after killing the High Priest of Scabaras

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: Have you freed the High Priest of Scabaras yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: You should do so soon.

sections.title: Talking to the High Priest of Scabaras
Talking to the High Priest again before learning about Pharaoh Kemesis

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any other information about Amascut that could help me?

lines.speaker: High Priest of Scabaras
lines.text: We were not the only ones to fall to the Devourer. She has control over Pharaoh Kemesis of Menaphos.

sections.title: Talking to the High Priest after saying the incantation

lines.speaker: High Priest of Scabaras
lines.text: What... What happened?

sections.title: Talking to the High Priest again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: High Priest of Scabaras
lines.text: Hello, adventurer. Thank you for freeing me from the Devourer. I would be alone now. Scabaras teaches us to remain isolated, and I have much to think about.

lines.speaker: High Priest of Scabaras
lines.text: Stay back!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Majaan, Manand, Almuk.

lines.speaker: High Priest of Scabaras
lines.text: Argh!

lines.speaker: High Priest of Scabaras
lines.text: What... What happened?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's alright. You were being controlled by Amascut, but you're free now.

lines.speaker: High Priest of Scabaras
lines.text: I... I don't remember.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've been in your position. Don't worry, your memories will come back in time. Familiar places and objects can help them come back faster.

lines.speaker: High Priest of Scabaras
lines.text: This room is familiar... as I look around, I feel something stirring...

lines.speaker: High Priest of Scabaras
lines.text: Yes, I can see it now! There are a few gaps, but it's all coming back to me. Thank you, adventurer.

lines.speaker: High Priest of Scabaras
lines.text: There is much for me to set right. We have made some terrible mistakes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know the feeling. What did she have you do?

lines.speaker: High Priest of Scabaras
lines.text: Many things. I have been under her control my whole life. It was the same for my predecessor, and her predecessor as well. We were all slaves to the Devourer, even our lord himself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: She controls Scabaras?

lines.speaker: High Priest of Scabaras
lines.text: Yes. She used him as her puppet for centuries, but now she has a new plan for him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What new plan?

lines.speaker: High Priest of Scabaras
lines.text: I don't know. She took him into some tomb, along with the others.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Osmumten's tomb. Who are these others you speak of?

lines.speaker: High Priest of Scabaras
lines.text: Apmeken, Crondis and Het. The other avatars of Tumeken.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: She has them all?

lines.speaker: High Priest of Scabaras
lines.text: She does. We helped her capture them. It took many lifetimes, but she was able to fool them all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: This isn't good. There're weapons in that tomb that can transfer divine power between beings. She must be planning to use them on the lesser deities.

lines.speaker: High Priest of Scabaras
lines.text: The four of them all came from Tumeken himself. Their power is his power. If she claims that power as her own, she will become a true goddess...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Don't worry, I'll make sure that doesn't happen.

lines.speaker: High Priest of Scabaras
lines.text: How can you hope to withstand her power?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll find a way. Do you have any other information that can help me?

lines.speaker: High Priest of Scabaras
lines.text: We were not the only ones to fall to the Devourer. She has control over Pharaoh Kemesis of Menaphos.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: We feared as much. Do you know what she wants with him?

lines.speaker: High Priest of Scabaras
lines.text: She needed control over the city, but she kept the reason a secret. She even had us release locusts and frogs into Sophanem so she could have Menaphos locked down.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You caused the plagues? The cows and the pox as well?

lines.speaker: High Priest of Scabaras
lines.text: We did also poison the cows. I know nothing of this pox, though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... what will you do now?

lines.speaker: High Priest of Scabaras
lines.text: I fear many of my people are forever lost, but I will save as many as I can. Together, we will reform the Sect of Scabaras, and right the wrongs of the Devourer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you'll help me fight her?

lines.speaker: High Priest of Scabaras
lines.text: In our own way. Before the Devourer, we were an isolated people, just as Scabaras taught us to be. We will go back to that way of life.

lines.speaker: High Priest of Scabaras
lines.text: We may go unseen, but we will be there, fighting the forces of the Devourer wherever they appear.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good to hear. Now, I'd better head over to Nardah. I have a friend there who needs to hear about all of this.

lines.speaker: High Priest of Scabaras
lines.text: Good luck in your fight, adventurer. May Scabaras guide you.

sections.title: Talking to Mehhar after saying the incantation
Talking to Mehhar again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Mehhar.

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: Welcome back, adventurer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, Mehhar.

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: I sense that the Devourer's corruption has been lessened. I trust you were successful?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The High Priest is free.

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: You have my thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Does this mean you can rest now?

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: That was my hope, but while the High Priest is free, many scabarites have been lost to the Devourer. I sense that they will soon descend on my tomb, hoping to reclaim it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there anything I can do to help?

lines.speaker: Mehhar
lines.text: Continue your fight against the Devourer. Only with her defeat, will we finally find freedom.

sections.title: Cure Me Pox
Talking to Maisa or Zahur
Talking to Zahur or Maisa after interrupting the previous conversation

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: So what did you find in those ruins?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found the High Priest of Scabaras there, under Amascut's control. Turns out she had him and the scabarites cause the plagues in Sophanem.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: They caused the plagues? Why?

sections.title: Talking to Zahur again

lines.speaker: Zahur
lines.text: Hello again. Do you need anything?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where can I get a Lily of the Elid?

lines.speaker: Zahur
lines.text: There's a small island just west of here where you should be able to find some.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Ah, there you are. Any luck in those ruins?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Plenty. I discovered all sorts of things. How about you?

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: I filled Zahur in on our discoveries and obtained a blood sample from one of Sophanem's citizens for her. Don't ask me how. She's looked over it, and thinks the pox should be easily curable.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great!

lines.speaker: Zahur
lines.text: Yes, it's a very interesting one this. Do you know of the cadava bush?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes! One time I used some to help this young couple. I gave one of them a cadava potion to appear dead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It all went a bit wrong though, and he ended up with her cousin instead...

lines.speaker: Zahur
lines.text: Right...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, and I used one another time when I was helping My Arm!

lines.speaker: Zahur
lines.text: You were helping your arm? I'm not sure I need to know about your private life...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, not my arm. My Arm! He's a troll! He needed to kill an old man, so we used a potion to fake it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It all went a bit wrong though... again. That seems to happen quite often for some reason...

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: What on Gielinor are you on about?

lines.speaker: Zahur
lines.text: Maybe we should get back to what I was saying...

lines.speaker: Zahur
lines.text: As most people know, cadava berries are quite poisonous. When used in the right quantity, they can put someone into a deep sleep. Use the wrong amount...

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: ... and you're not going to be waking up again. Cadava potions are standard issue for Al Kharid spies for that very reason. In fact, they're what I used to get myself and those citizens out of Menaphos.

lines.speaker: Zahur
lines.text: Well, the thing about cadava berries is that they're not the only part of the bush that's dangerous. If you're not careful, the bark can have quite a nasty effect on the skin.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Like the spots from the pox! Are you saying that's what caused all the trouble? A rogue cadava bush?

lines.speaker: Zahur
lines.text: Maybe, but you won't find any of those bushes this far south. It's quite unexpected. I don't think anyone in Sophanem would even recognise cadava bark, let alone figure out it caused the pox.

lines.speaker: Zahur
lines.text: To be honest, I probably wouldn't have suspected it myself either. I only realised because of the time I spent training with the Apothecary in Varrock.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: It sounds like someone could have caused the pox on purpose.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well that leads on to what I discovered in those ruins. I found the High Priest of Scabaras there, under Amascut's control. Turns out she had him and the scabarites cause the plagues in Sophanem.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: They caused the plagues? Why?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Amascut wanted an excuse to have Menaphos locked down. She's up to something there, but the High Priest of Scabaras didn't know what. However, he did confirm that Pharaoh Kemesis is under her control as well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The thing is, now that I think about it, the High Priest of Scabaras didn't actually seem to be aware of the pox. I doubt he'd have been able to get hold of cadava bark either.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: So there's other forces at play. That's not good. What else did you find out?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The next bit is worse, I'm afraid. I found out what Amascut is looking for in that tomb. Apparently, there are two weapons in there that could grant her true godhood.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: True godhood? You can't be serious!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid so. She's managed to capture all four of the lesser gods. It's likely that her plan is to use the weapons to claim their power.

lines.speaker: Zahur
lines.text: The lesser gods? It can't be!

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Are you really sure about all of this?

lines.speaker: Zahur
lines.text: Do not doubt the will of the Devourer, Maisa. She is a force of pure evil.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: It wasn't that long ago that I didn't even think she was still around. Now I'm hearing she's trying to become a true goddess! It's a lot to get used to.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I do have one bit of good news. I know an incantation that can free a person from her mind control. We can use it to free the people at the necropolis.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Right, well we'd better get to work then. First, let's finish off this cure. Once that's done, we can free the citizens.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anything you need me to do?

lines.speaker: Zahur
lines.text: Maisa has already obtained most of what I need. All that's left now is some scarab venom and a Lily of the Elid.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Okay, let's split up. I'll get the scarab venom.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And I guess that leaves the Lily of the Elid for me. From the name, I assume they grow near the river?

lines.speaker: Zahur
lines.text: That's right. I know of a small island just west of here where you can find some.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll be back soon.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: As will I.

sections.title: Crossing the river to the island
Crossing river again without meat
Crossing river while holding meat
Picking the lily

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there crocodile. I think I'll call you Roger. You're looking very hungry, Roger.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I'd better find something to distract you with before I try getting any closer.

sections.title: Returning to Zahur
Talking to Zahur before explaining the equipment

lines.speaker: Zahur
lines.text: I think I have everything I need to make the cure now.

lines.speaker: Zahur
lines.text: Welcome back. Do you have a Lily of the Elid?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet.

lines.speaker: Zahur
lines.text: Well, there's a small island just west of here where you can find some.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay. I'll be back soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here you go.

lines.speaker: Zahur
lines.text: I think I have everything I need to make the cure now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I help with anything else?

lines.speaker: Zahur
lines.text: Actually, I think you can. To make the cure, I'll need to get the ingredients to just the right temperature. While I get them prepared, could you get my equipment heated up?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I do that?

lines.speaker: Zahur
lines.text: Just adjust the individual heats until the overall temperature is at the marked level. Be warned, the equipment can be a bit temperamental sometimes.

sections.title: Talking to Maisa after Zahur explains the equipment
Talking to Zahur again

lines.speaker: Zahur
lines.text: Hello again. Do you need anything?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I need to do to this equipment?

lines.speaker: Zahur
lines.text: Just adjust the individual heats until the overall temperature is at the marked level. Be warned, the equipment can be a bit temperamental sometimes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Hey. You should help Zahur with her equipment.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay.

sections.title: Warming up the equipment
Talking to Zahur before getting the crate

lines.speaker: Zahur
lines.text: Hello again. Do you need anything?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have that first batch of cure for me?

lines.speaker: Zahur
lines.text: Yes, here it is. Let the High Priest know that I'll be sending down the rest once it's done.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

sections.title: Talking to Zahur after losing the crate

lines.speaker: Zahur
lines.text: Hello again. Do you need anything?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I lost that crate you gave me.

lines.speaker: Zahur
lines.text: You lost a whole crate? How?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't really know.

lines.speaker: Zahur
lines.text: Well I have the next batch ready now, so you can take that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

sections.title: Talking to Zahur again

lines.speaker: Zahur
lines.text: Hello again. Do you need anything?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where do I need to take this crate?

lines.speaker: Zahur
lines.text: It needs delivering to the High Priest of Icthlarin in Sophanem. Let him know that I'll be sending down the rest once it's done.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Zahur
lines.text: Excellent! I think I have it!

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Great!

lines.speaker: Zahur
lines.text: Now, it's going to take a few batches of this to cure everyone in Sophanem. I'll get started on those right away. In the meantime, I can give you the first batch to take down there now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay. I can deliver it to the High Priest of Icthlarin.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Meet me back at the necropolis once you're done.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Will do.

lines.speaker: Zahur
lines.text: Here's the first batch for you to take to the High Priest of Icthlarin. Let him know that I'll be sending down the rest once it's done.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

sections.title: Talking to the High Priest of Sophanem
Talking to the High Priest after giving the crate

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: What's this crate?

sections.title: Talking to the High Priest again

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Thank you for bringing us that cure. We will hopefully start seeing the effects soon.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sure it won't take long. I'll be back to see for myself once I'm done at the necropolis.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. How are things going here?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: We're making progress on those plagues. With our citizens back, we had enough people to finally drive out the frogs and locusts that were filling the town up.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: We worried they'd just come back, but perhaps we have managed to appease whichever god sent them.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: The infected cows have been dealt with as well. The only plague left now is this infuriating pox. Still, at least it isn't contagious.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I think I might be able to help with that.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: How?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh... I was meant to bring you something, but it seems I've forgotten it. I'll be right back.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: What's this crate?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If all goes well, the potions in there should cure the pox. Sophanem will finally be free of the plagues. This is just the first batch. More will come soon.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Free of the plagues? How is this possible? We tried everything!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well I just found a good herbalist. What did you try?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Er... mostly I prayed to Icthlarin and hoped for the best.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right... Well I have some things to do down at the necropolis. I'll be back to see how the potions are working once I'm done.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Thank you, friend. You are once again our saviour!

sections.title: Fight with the Menaphite Akh
Talking to Maisa at the necropolis
Talking to Maisa after the cutscene starts

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Looks like we have a welcome party.

sections.title: Inspecting the blocked entry after the citizens start mining
Talking to Maisa after the cutscene moves to Maisa's camp

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Let's head back to my camp.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Stop!

sections.title: Escape fire during the fight with the Menaphite Akh

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Hey there. Everything go okay with the High Priest?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think so. I guess we'll see for sure soon.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Well in the meantime, do you think this incantation will work?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It did when I used it on the High Priest of Scabaras, so we shouldn't have any issues here.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Let's not waste any more time then.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Looks like we have a welcome party.

lines.speaker: Guard
lines.text: You have been warned before... leave, now...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Amascut's hold on you all is at an end. Majaan, Manand, Almuk.

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: My... my head...

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: What happened?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You were all enthralled by the Devourer, but you're free now. Things will be confusing at the moment, but your memories will return in time.

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: The Devourer? It can't be!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm afraid so.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: You've all been through a lot, but unfortunately you won't be able to return to Menaphos. The city remains locked down.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: You should seek refuge in Sophanem or one of the other nearby settlements. Hopefully you'll be able to go home soon.

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: You saved us!

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: Thank you!

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Well... that's quite the blockage.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Clearing it is going to take work. A lot of work.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And time isn't on our side. Amascut is already in there looking for those weapons.

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: Excuse me...

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: Sorry to interrupt, but we can help.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: We can mine away the blockage for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you sure? You've already been out here so long.

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: The Devourer made us her slaves. We want to help you stop her.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Thank you.

lines.speaker: Citizen
lines.text: We'll get to work at once.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: It's still going to be a bit longer before the cure starts to work. I guess we should head back to my camp for now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Stop!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Stay quiet!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's going on?

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: I know you're here! Come out!

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Impressive, Maisa.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Osman! You've been spying on us!

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: That is the job of a spy, Maisa. I remember that, but it seems you do not.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: What's that supposed to mean?

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: We had a plan, Maisa. You were ordered to stay in Menaphos. Now your recklessness has cost us dearly.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Don't lecture me on cost. I know exactly how much this little game of yours has cost.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Yes... Kaleef. Did you think I wasn't aware of your little romance?

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Did you take me for a fool? I've been dealing in deception since before you were born. Yours was not hard to spot.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: I made the mistake of letting it slide. A poor move on my part. Kaleef's death has clearly impacted your ability to think rationally.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Or maybe it just made me see the truth! You're a monster, Osman! You'd see us all dead to get what you want!

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: I serve Al Kharid, Maisa, just as you once did.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: I'll never stop serving Al Kharid. I've just stopped serving you!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Soooo...

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: I hope you don't believe yourself innocent in all of this. You have been meddling in affairs that are not your concern.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've only been trying to help.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: By curing the pox? Tell me, who does that help? Al Kharid or Menaphos?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know, despite all your spying, Al Kharid and Menaphos aren't actually enemies.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: On paper perhaps, but our two cities were at war for years. Signing a few treaties does not stop us from being enemies.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Even if that's true, I don't see why the pox being cured is a problem.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: It will surely lead to the end of the Menaphos quarantine. That is not to our benefit. We wanted that city isolated.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There are larger forces at play here, Osman. That quarantine was helping them far more than it benefitted you.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: What are you on about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Amascut. She has control over Pharaoh Kemesis. She arranged the quarantine to keep her actions in Menaphos a secret.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's why we made the cure. We need to get in there and find out what she's up to.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: The Devourer? You speak of legends and fairy tales.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't believe the gods exist?

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Maybe once, but that was a long time ago.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're wrong, Osman. The Devourer's behind everything that's been happening down here, and the worst is yet to come. She's looking for some weapons. Ones that could grant her true godhood.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: And these weapons are in Menaphos?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No. She's after something else in Menaphos. We don't know what... yet. The weapons are in a tomb to the south, and she's already in there. She could be using them right now.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Pharaoh Kemesis has you fooled. This is clearly some ploy of his to keep us confused and divided. He is our true foe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Osman, you can't...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What the...

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: Time to go...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Osman! Don't you dare!

lines.speaker: Menaphite Akh
lines.text: The mistress has tired of your interference...

lines.speaker: Menaphite Akh
lines.text: Pray to your false gods... your end comes now...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great! Here we go again.

sections.title: Defeating the Menaphite Akh

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nice of you to help.

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: You had it all in hand. I must say, this has been an interesting development.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So do you believe Amascut is involved now?

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: No. I believe what my eyes tell me, and I am yet to see any true evidence that this is the work of an ancient goddess.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh you've got to be...

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: But I will admit that there is more going on here than I first thought. Dark magic is clearly at play. This may require us to reconsider our approach.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What does that even mean?

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: For now, nothing. I need to go and think on this. I suggest you seek out Maisa and see what results your meddling has caused. I'm sure I'll be in touch at some point.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Dare I ask for a 'thank you' this time?

lines.speaker: Osman
lines.text: I imagine Maisa will have gone to Sophanem to check on this cure. She will be waiting for you. Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's what I thought. Thanks, Osman.

sections.title: Talking to Maisa or the High Priest
Talking to the High Priest after the cutscene starts

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How long do you think we'll need to wait?

sections.title: Talking to High Priest before getting the reward

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Hello again! Please take this as a thank you for all your help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks.

sections.title: Search Tent before getting the reward

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Sorry for leaving you back there. How was your chat with Osman?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He seems to have been somewhat enlightened.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: After you left, we were attacked by one of Amascut's servants. I think they convinced Osman that there's more to all of this than he realised.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Maybe he'll finally get his head out of the sand. We don't need him, though. Look at what we've managed on our own.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, the cure. Is it working?

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: I'll let the High Priest answer that one.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Hello again, friend. Your cure has worked wonderfully. Not a single citizen is infected any more. We're finally free of the plagues!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And Menaphos?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: We sent word to them earlier. They have agreed to meet with us at the bridge. If all goes well, this quarantine will soon be behind us.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's hope so.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Please do join us for the meeting. After all, none of this would have been possible without you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How long do you think we'll need to wait?

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: I don't know. This has probably put Pharaoh Kemesis in an awkward spot. He'll have much to consider.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: If the quarantine benefits the Devourer, he won't want to end it. Problem is, if he doesn't, it will draw a lot of suspicion.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Makes you wonder how much control he actually has left. Does he make any decisions for himself now, or is it all just Amascut?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How are you doing, by the way?

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know, the stuff Osman said about you and Kaleef.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: What happened between Kaleef and I is none of your business.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If you're sure.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Speaking of Osman, that reminds me.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: He left this for you. I'll leave it in the tent at my camp for you to pick up.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: He left this for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: His way of saying 'thank you' perhaps?

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Doubt it. He's probably just trying to keep on your good side.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you think he's here?

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: If not him, one of his minions will be. He'll want to know what happens at this meeting.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Anyway, he wasn't the only one with something for you. This is from the High Priest.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: You can pick it up from him once we're done here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sure I'll be able to put it to good use.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, looks like something's happening.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Hang on... is that...?

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Pharaoh Kemesis! I did not expect you to attend to this personally.

lines.speaker: High Priest
lines.text: Pharaoh Kemesis?

lines.speaker: Pharaoh Kemesis
lines.text: The city of Menaphos will begin preparations to end the quarantine. It will take some time for these preparations to be complete. Good day.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well that was anticlimactic.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: That's one way of putting it. Still, I wasn't expecting Kemesis himself to make the announcement. It's very unusual for him to appear in public.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you think it means?

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: I don't know. Until we can get into Menaphos, I doubt we'll find out.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: He said it would take some time for the city to open.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Stalling for as long as possible, no doubt.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what now?

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: The citizens should hopefully be nearly done unblocking the entrance to Osmumten's Tomb. I'm going to head down there.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: As for you, I'd say you've earnt a small break. Take whatever time you need to prepare, then meet me at the tomb once you're ready.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Alright, I'll see you soon. Good luck.

sections.title: Historical dialogue
Quest ending before the release of Tombs of Amascut on 24 August 2022

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what now?

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: I think we've done all we can for the moment. Menaphos is preparing to reopen, and the citizens are unblocking the entrance to Osmumten's Tomb.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Until we see some progress in one of those areas, we'll just have to wait. Hopefully it won't be too long.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: In the meantime, I'll be helping out at the excavation. I think you'll agree that's our top priority.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Indeed.

lines.speaker: Maisa
lines.text: Look after yourself. You'll need to be well prepared for what's to come. I'll send word once the entry is cleared.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Good luck.
